risk_if_not_implemented: Without Conflict of Interests, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Third-Party Contract Requirements
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to require contractual requirements for applicable security, compliance and resilience requirements with third-parties, reflecting the organization's needs to protect its Technology Assets, Applications, Services and/or Data (TAASD).
compensating_control_1.justification: Third-Party Contract Requirements (TPM-05) provides third-party oversight that compensates for the absence of Conflict of Interests (TPM-04.3) by extending security obligations contractually and monitoring third-party risk in lieu of direct control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Third-Party Services
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to mitigate the risks associated with third-party access to the organization's Technology Assets, Applications, Services and/or Data (TAASD).
compensating_control_2.justification: Third-Party Services (TPM-04) provides third-party oversight that compensates for the absence of Conflict of Interests (TPM-04.3) by extending security obligations contractually and monitoring third-party risk in lieu of direct control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
